step_by_step_reasoning: Drone pilots process imagery via external software. AWS pipeline starts with CloudWatch Event schedule triggering Step Functions, orchestrating three sequential Lambdas: Lambda 1 checks Drone API; Lambda 2 uploads imagery to S3; Lambda 3 creates raster proxies pointing to S3 and writes them to an On-Prem database. Internal users access these images through On-Prem GIS software integrated with S3 via Storage Gateway.

graph.name: Marathon Oil Drone Imagery Processing Pipeline
graph.notes: Processes high-resolution well-pad drone imagery. Step Functions coordinate Lambdas serverlessly, and on-premises software reads directly from S3 via Storage Gateway using raster proxies.

nodes.name: CloudWatch Event
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Scheduled trigger initiating pipeline run.

nodes.name: Step Functions Orchestrator
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Coordinates execution of three distinct Lambdas.

nodes.name: Check Flight API Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Queries third-party API for newly uploaded flight data.

nodes.name: S3 Image Uploader Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Downloads files from drone software and writes to S3.

nodes.name: Raster Proxy Creator Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Generates raster proxy pointing to S3 and updates GIS Database.

nodes.name: S3 Image Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores ortho mosaic, terrain, and elevation models.

nodes.name: AWS Storage Gateway
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Translates local server file system calls to S3 API calls.

nodes.name: Third-Party Drone Imagery Host
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Holds initial drone flight images.

nodes.name: On-Prem GIS Database
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores spatial reference URLs (raster proxies).

nodes.name: On-Prem Mapping Server
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: On-prem server serving GIS software.

nodes.name: Internal Maintenance & Ops Teams
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Internal users querying well-pad imagery to spot issues.
